prep.summary: Prepare for your sync with Justin by addressing the unresolved ServiceNow POC pricing discrepancy—the vendor is pushing an unfavorable per-user model to hit a suspected $12M target. Partner with Jonathan on the requirements AI ask—you now have folder access and must use the existing prompts to flush out details. Note that no information was found in your work notes regarding the sent emails folder—you must check this source directly.
tasks: Resolve the ServiceNow POC pricing dispute—push back on the vendor's per-user model in favor of your event-based usage requirements.
Partner with Jonathan to process the gathered business requirements—utilize the existing AI prompts to flush out the necessary details.
Review your sent emails folder prior to the meeting—the local work notes contain no details on this item.
Finalize the TransUnion pilot contract—confirm Provina is handling the final execution steps.
prep.title: Bi-weekly 1:1 w/Justin White | Mark Francis

prep.summary: Search your sent emails folder to reconstruct the context around Scan.ai—your local work notes contain no mentions of this platform despite the upcoming D&S Transformation Tech Partner Connect. Analyze these past communications to map out previous decision points—particularly regarding how Scan.ai interfaces with your broader architecture. Present these historical email findings to the partners to establish a clear baseline for the integration roadmap.
tasks: Search your sent emails folder using the query 'Scan.ai' to locate previous discussion threads.
Extract key decisions and timeline commitments from those past emails to build a historical summary.
Reconcile the missing Scan.ai context in your 5/13 P&C Architecture work notes to bridge the documentation gap.
Draft a brief speaking agenda outlining Francis Inc.'s technical expectations for the Scan.ai partner integration.
prep.title: D&S Transformation Tech Partner Connect
